rules_fired.because: Concentrated corrosive reagents (HCl, TFA, NaOH) release significant heat on dilution. Adding water to a concentrated corrosive can boil the liquid and cause spattering; always add the corrosive slowly to water. TFA and HCl volatilise in the hood — decant and dilute in a ventilated space. Neutralise to pH 6–8 before drain disposal per local regulations.
rules_fired.cite: Sigma-Aldrich HCl Safety Note 258148. ACS Safety in Academic Chemistry Laboratories, 7th ed., Ch. 4. EPA 40 CFR Part 261 (hazardous waste neutralisation).
